The Exclusively Empowered Callout (Completed 05/07/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 1 of 3
Speaker 1: Hey, guys. Welcome to the first Themeplate. This is going to be the Exclusively
Empowered Call Out. You'll see right next to it, we have this little notation of MVF1.
That is minimum viable funnel number one. You're going to want to pay attention to
those, especially if you're going to be doing those email only launches. These are the
ones that I would suggest writing to create that minimum viable funnel.
Anyway, this is the Themeplate you use to invite prospects on to the journey, and more
importantly, to get them excited about it through that sense of exclusive empowerment
that we've talking about throughout this module, and we also answered some questions
on it.
It's a simple Themeplate. It's essentially going to go like this. You can open up
announcing with a link what it is you're going to be inviting them to. Then you could
suggest that big outcome, that big outcome of your webinar series or launch, whatever
it is. What are you promising? What is going to be the outcome of that? Then what is
that moment of highest tension? We did that exercise in module two, but to really
motivate someone to enter this journey, you got to light that fire. The moment of
highest tension is what turns that scrape into a cut, the paper cut into an infected
wound, and so forth. You really want to agitate it by just showing what that moment of
highest tension is.
Then you could have your second call to action, that once again invites them. Right after
that moment of highest tension, right when you've agitated that pain and made it
present, and urgent, and something they need to escape, that's a good time to drop a
second invitation. Then you're going to build up some interest around the event, some
curiosity before ending with that exclusively empowered close. This is where you set
that bar of what they need to have in place. What are the prerequisites for them getting
the most of this event, this webinar, this series, this launch, whatever it is. Allow them
to cash in those privilege points.
An example of this is something that you may have seen. This one is pulled straight from
our own recent launch. This is a preliminary draft, so it may have looked a little different
from what you've seen. But in any event, it should look familiar.
Hey, I just wanted to reach out real quick. Tomorrow we'll be kicking off our brand new
Profit and Launch workshop. You're going to learn all about how to quickly write those
crazy good, high converting emails that can make or break your funnel or course launch.
Here right off the bat, we're leading with that big outcome, the type of emails that look
harmless enough on a funnel map until you realize those harrowing little bastards
actually need tender loving care to grow and mature into automated revenue-
generating bad asses, the types of emails that cause you to drunk post messages on
Facebook at 3:00 AM, hoping that one of your entrepreneurial friends will magically
refer you to a unicorn, a.k.a., a talented copywriter with immediate availability and
won't force you to take a reverse mortgage, the kind that after realizing you can tire out,
forces you to push back your launch date by eight weeks while you rack up 361 star
points at the local Starbucks and get to know every barista by name.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 3 --

The Exclusively Empowered Callout (Completed 05/07/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 2 of 3
This is all getting super hyper specific about what that pain is. That is the moment of
highest tension. When you can reflect that back right off the bat, you're really building
that trust and that presupposition that you know a better way. Not a fan of Starbucks
Rewards. Don't miss this workshop, [inaudible 00:03:43]. This is this call to action right
after that moment of highest tension. This is a fun way to frame your call to actions. It
doesn't always have to be show up, or click here, or join the workshop. Sometimes you
could actually frame it in a way that's included in the story you're telling.
The other thing I want to talk about here is just get hyper specific with things that are
familiar to your prospect, whether that's Starbucks Reward points. That's something we
could all really relate to. It just makes it feel like what you're offering matches that
reality area, your prospect's reality.
Then we have a quick sign-off and a PS. Is this a can't miss? Honestly, I have no clue. You
tell me. I mean, I wouldn't offer you an extra ticket to a Radiohead show unless I knew
for certain that you enjoyed listless walks in the rain and waxing philosophical over
existentialism and other topics that make the average person's head spin. Same goes
here.
We're going to pack this 60-minute show with an epic set list, play the crowd pleasers
like how copywriting has changed over the last few years and how to take full advantage
of those recent shifts in your next launch. These are just going to be points to sell the
event. I'm not going to read through them all right now.
But essentially, what we are doing is we aren't assuming that they're perfect for it.
We're actually being very up front. I do not know if this is going to be perfect for you.
This is what you're going to get. Those are the benefits, so we're selling the event.
Then here is where we get into the exclusive empowerment. You should only make it to
the show if, you should only show up if, you should only join my webinar if, so that's the
lead-in to what an exclusive empowered customer will be. Where can we give them a
chance to cash in, I was going to say their Starbucks points, but their privilege points,
their exclusivity points.
If you're an online entrepreneur, coach, consultant, expert, or influencer, who already
has a product you're crazy proud of or will in the near future and want to be able to 2X,
3X, or 5X sales with powerful and easy to implement launch copy, this be me. I'm in.
You're an established email or launch copywriter who charges the big bucks to do it for
the people above and wants to charge the even bigger bucks by getting better results in
less time, damn, it's like you read my mind.
Then we just close off the whole Starbucks joke. You won't miss racking up the
Starbucks points, knowing full well you'll be able to afford your own [inaudible 00:06:17]
five shot, flat whites. Thank you very much. I don't know why I said it that way. Probably
because that's how I order my own five shot, flat white lattes. But anyway, so that is
where you include those exclusive empowerment items, if you will, and really give them
a chance to cash in on those points.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

The Exclusively Empowered Callout (Completed 05/07/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 3 of 3
An advanced an optional strategy is this could be a way to segment your different
buckets, what we did here. The first call out was for online entrepreneurs, coaches, and
consultants. The second one was for copywriters. The third one was for just people who
want to buy their own lattes. We did not do anything with that bucket, but maybe we
could. Maybe we're going to strike up a partnership with Starbucks or something.
But anyway, that is the first email, that first Themeplate. I really want you to explore this
and make sure you have your exclusive empowerment points down, because that is
really the crux of this one, that plus the moment of highest tension. Give that a go.
Then I will meet you in the next Themeplate, which is going to be borrowing a little help
from a famous little Canadian pop star. I'll see you there in a sec.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
